title: The Proving Ground
covenant: A border march where every road answers to the Duchy and every hearth in Larkspur Vale keeps one unpaid debt alive. The pass wants closing before the first snow, and the woman with the key-shaped burn is the only one who remembers why.
styleBible: Romantic dark-fantasy oil painting with gold-leaf light, deep atmospheric perspective, expressive faces, and restrained PG-13 peril.

hero.name: Maren
hero.bearing: Weather-worn leathers over a courier's frame, dark hair bound back with waxed cord, and a road-warden's longbow carried like a habit.
hero.background: A former road-warden of the Vale who can hear when a path is lying.
hero.mark: a burn in the shape of a key

narration_blocks.text: Larkspur Vale wakes under a thin gold rain, and the bell above the waystation rings twice for a rider nobody sent. Maren reads the road the way other people read letters, and this morning the road is lying.

Three figures wait by the hearth-house: Edda, who keeps the Vale's fires and its grudges; Corin Voss, the Duchy's envoy with dust still on his seal-case; and behind the rain, a shape the old maps refuse to name — Vessarine, the Hollow Queen.

arc.title: The Proving Ground
arc.evil_plot: Vessarine, the Hollow Queen, is calling in every debt in the march at once, so the Vale must sell its roads to the Duchy before the snow seals the pass.
arc.stakes: The Vale's hearths, the road home, and one unpaid restitution.
arc.style_bible: Romantic dark-fantasy oil painting with gold-leaf light, deep atmospheric perspective, expressive faces, and restrained PG-13 peril.

cast_add.name: Vessarine
cast_add.visual: A tall pale sovereign in a gown of tarnished silver scales, a cracked porcelain half-mask over the left side of her face, ash-white hair worked into a crown of iron pins, and a mantle of crow feathers that drinks the light around her.
cast_add.voice: A cold contralto that never hurries, each word placed like a coin on a debt ledger.
cast_add.goal: Call in every debt in the march before the first snow.
cast_add.secret: The key-shaped burn on Maren's wrist matches the lock Vessarine cannot open.

cast_add.name: Edda
cast_add.visual: A broad-shouldered elder with white hair braided in a crown, flour-dusted forearms, a shawl the color of banked embers, and eyes that have out-waited three bad winters.
cast_add.voice: A warm, cracked alto that turns every sentence into a hearth story.
cast_add.goal: Keep the Vale's fires lit and its debts remembered.
cast_add.secret: She holds the written proof of Corin's debt sewn into her shawl hem.

cast_add.name: Corin Voss
cast_add.visual: A travel-lean man in the Duchy's slate-blue riding coat, brass seal-case at his hip, close-cropped dark beard, and gloved hands that never stray far from his ledger.
cast_add.voice: A careful tenor, courteous to the exact edge of honesty.
cast_add.goal: Close the pass road deal before the snow.
cast_add.secret: He owes Edda restitution he cannot pay without the Duchy noticing.

item_add.name: The ferry ledger
item_add.note: Every crossing the Vale still owes, in Edda's tight hand.

purse.reason: Road-warden's back pay counted out at the waystation

region_add.name: Larkspur Vale
region_add.visual: A terraced river vale of slate-roofed hearth-houses, larkspur meadows climbing to a ruined watchtower, and a gold-leafed old road stitching the terraces together under soft northern light.

turns.player: I ask Edda about Corin's debt.

narration_blocks.speaker: Edda
narration_blocks.text: “The debt stands, girl. Corin Voss swore restitution on this very hearth two winters back, and the hearth remembers longer than the Duchy does.”
Corin Voss studies his gloves while Edda speaks, and the lantern oath Maren swore on the old road feels suddenly heavier — kept now, and done with, the way a true promise ends.

purse.reason: Paid the road toll at the north gate

region_add.name: The Duchy
region_add.visual: A walled upland seat of slate towers and counting-houses, banners of slate-blue and brass over the toll gates, and straight paved roads that leave nothing to chance.

turns.player: I whisper what the road told me.

narration_blocks.text: THE STRUCK SENTENCE hangs in the hearth-house air, and every fire in the Vale leans away from it.

turns.player: We march for the pass at dawn.

narration_blocks.speaker: Corin Voss
narration_blocks.text: “Dawn, then. The Duchy's road is straight, but the Vale's is older — and I have learned this week which one of them lies less.”
Packs are weighed, the hearth is banked, and the larkspur meadows fold into blue shadow as the second day closes on the march.

turns.player: I check the wind before we leave.

narration_blocks.speaker: Edda
narration_blocks.text: “Wind's from the Duchy. It usually is, this time of year — take bread, and take the debt with you.”

narration_blocks.speaker: Corin Voss
narration_blocks.text: “The pass won't wait on weather, ranger. We leave within the hour — debts and all.”
Day three opens clear and cold over Larkspur Vale, and somewhere north the Hollow Queen's ledgers turn a page.

turns.player: We take the Duchy road at last.

narration_blocks.text: The toll gates rise out of morning haze, slate-blue banners snapping over paved stone, and the road that lied in the Vale runs honest and hard beneath the horses now.

narration_blocks.speaker: Corin Voss
narration_blocks.text: “Welcome to the Duchy, ranger. Mind the counting-houses — here even the wind pays toll.”
